methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
SemiQon is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Himadri Majumdar that has raised €24M+. The quantum computing sector encompasses a comp universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method was not applicable to this valuation. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers total, with zero companies matching SemiQon's same stage, one at an adjacent stage, and zero in a comparable valuation tier. This fell below the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for a reliable peer-median analysis. An all-sector median approach would introduce excessive noise and was rejected as methodologically unsound.

Given the data constraints, traditional comparable-company triangulation cannot be executed with sufficient rigor. The extreme stage and technology heterogeneity within quantum computing—spanning different modalities, commercialization timelines, and funding trajectories—makes broad-based benchmarking unreliable. Any valuation output at this stage would require either (1) a narrower set of actual transaction precedents, (2) bottom-up DCF assumptions tied to SemiQon's specific technical roadmap and market opportunity, or (3) a weighted assessment of the identified private peers with explicit stage and technology adjustments. The absence of viable comparable guidance underscores the early-stage, high-uncertainty character of frontier-tech valuation.
